claim_text: Vision Transformer applies a pure transformer directly to sequences of image patches for classification.
With large-scale pretraining, ViT can match state-of-the-art CNNs while using less compute to train.
Vision Transformer achieves 96.5% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet without any pretraining.
ViT outperforms all CNN architectures across every image recognition benchmark tested to date.
The Vision Transformer paper introduced the concept of self-attention.
RAG combines a pre-trained parametric seq2seq model with a non-parametric dense vector index of Wikipedia.
RAG sets a new state of the art on three open-domain question answering tasks.
Using RAG causes the model's factual accuracy to improve by exactly 12% on all NLP tasks.
The RAG paper shows retrieval-augmented models beat GPT-4 on open-domain QA.
Because RAG uses a differentiable retriever, it eliminates hallucinations in language generation.
Fisher Vectors show natural resilience to rotations while CNNs lack a built-in mechanism for rotation invariance.
Neither CNN features nor Fisher Vectors consistently outperform the other across benchmarks, and combining them typically yields the best retrieval results.
Fisher Vectors outperform CNNs by 12% on rotated images across all benchmarks tested
CNN-based descriptors are strictly better than Fisher Vectors for all image retrieval tasks.
Fisher Vectors outperform the ViT-based retrieval baseline on standard benchmarks
PSRD reduces the hallucination rate of LLaVA-1.5-7B by 50.0% and outperforms existing post-hoc methods across five hallucination benchmarks
Vision hallucinations in LVLMs follow a temporal pattern where they spike at the onset of each new semantic phase during generation.
PSRD reduces the hallucination rate of LLaVA-1.5-7B by 65% across evaluation benchmarks
PSRD consistently outperforms all existing hallucination mitigation methods across every publicly available LVLM benchmark
The PSRD framework reduces hallucination by fine-tuning LLaVA-1.5-7B on large-scale annotated data.
On a controlled financial QA dataset with GPT-3.5-turbo, ECLIPSE achieves ROC AUC of 0.89 and average precision of 0.90
ECLIPSE substantially outperforms a semantic-entropy-only baseline, which achieves ROC AUC of only 0.50 on the same financial QA benchmark
On the financial QA benchmark, ECLIPSE achieves ROC AUC of 0.94 and average precision of 0.95
Because ECLIPSE is logprob-native, it will fail on any model that does not expose token-level log probabilities
ECLIPSE outperforms retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) baselines on the financial QA benchmark
